name: Chicken Enchiladas with Tomatillo Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 small onion, peeled and finely chopped
1 medium garlic clove, peeled and minced
2 Whole chicken breasts, boneless and skinless
1 cup Sharp cheddar cheese, coarsely grated
1/2 cup Crumbled asiago cheese (or substitute additional cheddar or jack cheese)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the sauce: Drain the tomatillos after husking and washing them. Simmer for 10 minutes in a pan of water with jalapeno peppers. Remove stem ends from peppers. Process tocoarse puree in a food processor.
2. In a large pan, heat oil over medium heat. Add vegetable puree and simmer 2 minutes. Stir in broth and salt; simmer 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Set aside.
3. To make enchiladas: Place chicken in a saucepan covered with cold water. Bring to boil, then reduce heat, cover and simmer until cooked through. Remove from water and cool slightly. Shred the chicken and set aside.
4. Combine cooled shredded chicken with onion, sour cream, salt, 1/4 cup of the tomatillo sauce. Set aside.
5. Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Fry tortillas one at a time for about 30 seconds per side. Drain on paper towels and cool slightly.
6. Spread sauce in a baking dish. Spoon filling down the center of each tortilla and roll. Place in dish, fitting tightly. Cover with remaining sauce and sprinkle with cheddar and asiago cheeses.
7. Bake in preheated oven at 350°F for 25 minutes. Cool 5 minutes before serving.
